videos.description:
✅ Here’s the exact list I share with friends visiting Chicago (my free guide): https://bit.ly/Chicago-Guide-Camden

These 40 tips to know before visiting Chicago are what I wish I knew before my trip to Chicago. Hopefully these travel tips will help you from making some of my mistakes when visiting Chicago!

☕️ Here's the Instagram account I mentioned: https://bit.ly/cuppachicago

Every Tuesday, I share a travel tip you’ll actually use — like advice from a friend who travels nonstop -- https://bit.ly/TheTuesdayTravelTip

DISCLAIMER: I only express my opinion based on my experience. Your experience may be different. Links above may include affiliate commission or referrals. I'm part of an affiliate network and I receive compensation from partnering websites. This video is accurate as of the posting date but may not be accurate in the future.
videos.transcript: I used to live in Chicago and it's one of the most misunderstood cities in the world. So, my goal here is to set you up for success. We're going to cover a lot. Going to talk about some of the most common mistakes people make, talk about overrated and underrated spots, some food recommendations, and areas of town to stay in versus where you should actually hang out cuz there's some important distinctions there. So, the first mistake people make is not realizing that the time of year that you visit has such a drastic impact on how you engage with this city. You could make an argument that for 3 months out of the year, this is the best city in the world. And for the rest of the year, oh my gosh, it's maybe not even top 25. Like, the weather has such an impact. So, be cognizant of that. If you're visiting in the summer to early fall, it's going to be easy to enjoy. But if you're visiting during the winter, don't expect blue skies. expect things to be cold and just readjust expectations. You're going to want to have a lot more indoor activities planned compared to the summer and fall where you're going to want to be outside. Another really important thing to know is that the public transit here is probably the best you're going to find anywhere in the US. It is fantastic. Our main system is called the L and it works like a charm. It's so connected throughout the city and it really is safe and we'll touch on more safety stuff in a minute, but don't let anybody deter you from using it. It really should be your go-to method of getting around town. And don't forget to recognize that this isn't just a subway underground. You're going to see it all over the city, and it is beautiful. So, take a minute to recognize it and just appreciate it. And look, I get that using a new public transit system can be scary, but we've really simplified the system here. You no longer have to buy like a card or special ticket. Now, you can just tap right in and out with your digital wallet on your phone or with your credit card. It really is worth your time. Now, another common mistake that people make is they pick the wrong airport. Everybody seems to talk about O'Hare, O'Hare, O'Hare. And I get it. It's the bigger airport. It is the international airport. But if you're looking for convenience and are flying domestic, you should probably fly into Midway if you're planning to use public transit to get into the city. O'Hare, it's about a 50-minute ride into the loop, which is what we call the center of the city. And from Midway, it's only about 25 minutes. So you can save yourself a lot of time and a lot of distress if you fly into Midway where it's just a lot less packed. So keep that in mind. Another mistake that some people make is not realizing how walkable the city is and how much that actually changes the experience. So yes, rely on that public transit when you're going longer distances, but don't be afraid of walking. The architecture in the city is beautiful and it can be such a rewarding experience to just schedule some time to walk from point A to point B and leave yourself some of those long gaps to do so. And yes, of course, you can always Uber or lift, but I really think you'll be rewarded if you walk. And don't worry too much about the safety. I think media likes to overhype the safety concerns in this city. And look, I'm somebody who moves to a new country every single month. I've been to basically every big city within the US. Chicago is no different than anywhere else. There are pockets of crime, but if you're a tourist, you're not going to end up anywhere near those spots. 15 best things to do in Chicago.

Wondering what to do in Chicago? Our Chicago travel guide covers the top Chicago attractions, must-try food, favorite museums and a few local hidden gems.

Chicago is the best big city in the U.S.and you'll see why. From the iconic Chicago architecture river cruise to the world-famous Deep Dish Pizza, we’ve curated the perfect Chicago itinerary to save you time and money.
